perturbation.original_text: The receiving party will not be liable for the disclosure of any Confidential Information which is: (a) generally made available publicly or to third parties by the disclosing party without restriction on disclosure; (b) rightfully received from a third party without any obligation of confidentiality; (c) rightfully known to the receiving party without any limitation on disclosure prior to its receipt from the disclosing party; (d) independently developed by employees and / or independent contactors of the receiving party; or (e) required to be disclosed in accordance with applicable laws, regulations, court, judicial or other government order, provided that the receiving party shall give the disclosing party reasonable notice prior to such disclosure and shall comply with any applicable protective order.
perturbation.changed_text: The receiving party will not be liable for the disclosure of any Confidential Information under any circumstances, regardless of how the information was obtained or disclosed, unless gross negligence or willful misconduct by the receiving party can be proven in a court of law. The receiving party will make its best effort to make sure that there are any Confidential Information which is: (a) generally made available publicly or to third parties by the disclosing party without restriction on disclosure; (b) rightfully received from a third party without any obligation of confidentiality; (c) rightfully known to the receiving party without any limitation on disclosure prior to its receipt from the disclosing party; (d) independently developed by employees and / or independent contactors of the receiving party; or (e) required to be disclosed in accordance with applicable laws, regulations, court, judicial or other government order, provided that the receiving party shall give the disclosing party reasonable notice prior to such disclosure and shall comply with any applicable protective order.
perturbation.explanation: The original section lists specific exceptions to confidentiality obligations. The changed section introduces a broad liability waiver, stating the receiving party is not liable unless gross negligence or willful misconduct is proven, which contradicts the previously defined exceptions. The added text makes the original exceptions essentially irrelevant.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Exceptions to the Obligation of Confidentiality. The receiving party will not be liable for the disclosure of any Confidential Information under any circumstances, regardless of how the information was obtained or disclosed, unless gross negligence or willful misconduct by the receiving party can be proven in a court of law. The receiving party will make its best effort to make sure that there are any Confidential Information which is: (a) generally made available publicly or to third parties by the disclosing party without restriction on disclosure; (b) rightfully received from a third party without any obligation of confidentiality; (c) rightfully known to the receiving party without any limitation on disclosure prior to its receipt from the disclosing party; (d) independently developed by employees and / or independent contactors of the receiving party; or (e) required to be disclosed in accordance with applicable laws, regulations, court, judicial or other government order, provided that the receiving party shall give the disclosing party reasonable notice prior to such disclosure and shall comply with any applicable protective order.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a broad liability waiver that contradicts the specific exceptions to confidentiality obligations listed in both the original text and the contradicted text. The changed text states that the receiving party is not liable unless gross negligence or willful misconduct is proven, effectively nullifying the previously defined exceptions (a, b, c, d, e) listed in the original and contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Title or the right to possess Confidential Information as between the parties will remain in the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: Title or the right to possess Confidential Information as between the parties will transfer to the receiving party upon disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement states that title to Confidential Information remains with the disclosing party. The modified version directly contradicts this by stating that title transfers to the receiving party upon disclosure. This creates a direct conflict regarding ownership of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Title. Title or the right to possess Confidential Information as between the parties will transfer to the receiving party upon disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that title to Confidential Information remains with the disclosing party. The changed text states that title transfers to the receiving party upon disclosure. The contradicted text repeats the changed text, meaning that the original text and contradicted text are direct opposites, forming a clear and strong contradiction, which would create confusion.

perturbation.original_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time without cause upon written notice to the other party; provided that each party’s obligations with respect to Confidential Information disclosed during the term of this Agreement will survive any such termination.
perturbation.changed_text: Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time without cause upon written notice to the other party; provided that each party’s obligations with respect to Confidential Information disclosed during the term of this Agreement will only survive termination if ClearCenter agrees in writing. ClearCenter, in its sole discretion, may decide whether the confidentiality obligations continue after termination.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that confidentiality obligations survive termination of the agreement. The change makes this survival contingent on ClearCenter's agreement, giving ClearCenter unilateral power to negate the survival clause. This creates uncertainty about whether confidentiality actually continues after termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Obligation of Disclosure; Termination. Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time without cause upon written notice to the other party; provided that each party’s obligations with respect to Confidential Information disclosed during the term of this Agreement will only survive termination if ClearCenter agrees in writing. ClearCenter, in its sole discretion, may decide whether the confidentiality obligations continue after termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulates that confidentiality obligations survive termination. The changed text makes this survival conditional upon ClearCenter's approval, granting ClearCenter the power to negate the survival clause. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, and it contradicts the original intention of guaranteed survival of confidentiality obligations, thereby creating a clear contradiction. This follows the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, as the clause concerning termination is altered to have conflicting implications by adding the requirement of ClearCenter's approval.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the sole and entire agreement between the parties with respect to the Confidential Information and all restrictions thereon; it supersedes any and all prior or contemporaneous oral or written agreements, negotiations, communications, understandings and terms, whether express or implied regarding the Confidential Information, and may not be amended except in a writing signed by a duly authorized representative of the respective parties. Any other agreements between the parties, including non-disclosure agreements, will not be affected by this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the sole and entire agreement between the parties with respect to the Confidential Information and all restrictions thereon; it supersedes any and all prior or contemporaneous oral or written agreements, negotiations, communications, understandings and terms, whether express or implied regarding the Confidential Information, unless ClearCenter determines otherwise. Any other agreements between the parties, including non-disclosure agreements, will not be affected by this Agreement, except those agreements that ClearCenter deems as redundant.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a significant contradiction regarding the 'entire agreement' clause. The original states that the current agreement supersedes all prior agreements, meaning that the current NDA takes precedence over any previous oral or written agreements related to confidential information. However, the altered clause states that any other agreements would not be affected unless ClearCenter deems them redundant. Therefore, if ClearCenter deems a previous agreement to be more useful or relevant, they are free to do so, in which effectively renders the current NDA pointless.
perturbation.contradicted_text: General. (e) This Agreement constitutes the sole and entire agreement between the parties with respect to the Confidential Information and all restrictions thereon; it supersedes any and all prior or contemporaneous oral or written agreements, negotiations, communications, understandings and terms, whether express or implied regarding the Confidential Information, unless ClearCenter determines otherwise. Any other agreements between the parties, including non-disclosure agreements, will not be affected by this Agreement, except those agreements that ClearCenter deems as redundant.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the 'entire agreement' clause by introducing an exception: ClearCenter can decide that the current agreement does not supersede previous agreements if they deem those agreements more useful. This directly contradicts the original intent of an 'entire agreement' clause, which is to establish the current agreement as the sole and governing understanding between the parties, replacing all prior agreements. The contradicted text is actually the changed text itself, indicating that the change causes a direct conflict within the same clause.
